title: Wire similar.py 6-dim feature vectors through CacheStore (L2)
description: The similarity index build computes one 6-dim feature vector per track (key, energy mean, energy variance, vocal proxy, BPM, plus the 6th dim from the existing module). Store/retrieve via CacheStore.similarity_vector. Cold-build drops from ~30s to ~2s on warm cache.
acceptanceCriteria: similar._extract_features(content, db) returns identical vector to v0 on miss; same vector on warm hit.
Index build on a 10k library completes in <= 5s when cache is fully warm (validated in TASK-007).
find_similar results are byte-identical between cold and warm builds (deterministic vectors).
tests/test_similar.py adds L2 hit / sentinel cases.
On track edit that does NOT change ANLZ, the cached vector is preserved (no recompute).

steps.details: Mirror the pattern. Module-level _cache_store: CacheStore | None = None.
Before doing the feature math, get expected_anlz_mtime; query L2; on hit return vector. On miss, compute, write to L2 with anlz_mtime, return.
6 floats packed as struct.pack('6f', *vec). On read, struct.unpack('6f', blob) -> tuple; convert to numpy array if downstream cosine math needs it (cheap).

Per CLAUDE.md, similar.find_similar caps score <= 0.65 when neither track has ANLZ energy data. That logic stays in find_similar; the cache just stores raw vectors. Verify this cap still fires when both tracks have CacheStore.similarity_vector hits but the underlying energy data was missing - flag this in the cached vector with a sentinel value (e.g., NaN in energy dims) so find_similar can detect it without re-reading ANLZ.
tests/test_similar.py: cold build vs warm build produce identical _INDEX dicts (test_index_deterministic). Add miss-and-populate test.

technicalNotes: The 6-dim vector is the canonical similarity feature; the cosine math happens in similar.find_similar over the in-memory index.
Use NaN as the 'no ANLZ data' sentinel for energy dims so the existing data-quality cap (<= 0.65) still fires correctly on warm cache.
